no conviction. Why? Because that means we've gotten hard. We've gotten hard. I don't have
any conviction anymore. That's a problem. It's a good thing when we're sensitive to
the leadership of the Holy Spirit of God in our lives. And we respond, we yield. You know,
the Bible says something very interesting. We won't take time to go there, but in Daniel,
when the Bible gives us the record of the fiery furnace, Nebuchadnezzar says of those
three Hebrew children that they yielded themselves to the Lord. It's surrender is what it is.
We sing the song, I surrender. What does it say? All. Right? That's what the songwriter
said. When we sing that, are we being truthful? I surrender most. I surrender all. I surrender
all. Lord, I'm yours. I'm bought with a price. Whatever you want, I'm yielded to you, Lord.
But pastor, you don't understand. I already got my life planned out. I already got this
figured out, my 10-year plan. The people that I talked to, they told me I've got to develop
this plan and I've got all this. Let God be God. Let God be God and yield yourself. See,
if we want revival, and I ask the question, are you ready, ready for revival? Repent of
known sin. Ask God to show you, to make it known to you and repent. E, eliminate the
weights. Lay aside every weight and the sin which doth so easily beset us. A, agree with
God. I'll say yes, Lord, yes. When your spirit speaks to me with my whole heart, I'll agree.
I'm going to agree with God. D, draw nigh to God. Get into His presence. Spend time
with Him. When you walk in the light as He is in the light, man, have that fellowship,
that communion, that abiding. And then Y, yield to God. Just wave that white flag of
surrender. Lord, I want you to work in my heart. I want you to do something in my life.
God, I need revival. I'm ready for revival. I want to have those flames burning hot. I'm
ready for revival. Don't show up next Sunday and just think because it's on the calendar
it's going to happen. We've got to prepare. We've got to prepare so that we can be ready
for revival. I said this last week. We can have revival in our lives, personally, in
our church, corporately, before Mike Edwards ever gets here. It's not, he doesn't bring
it in a briefcase. Right? Brother Sommerdorf, when he goes to churches, he doesn't take
it in his briefcase and he opens it up and bam, we're going to have revival. It's not
how it works. It's not how it works. The people of God have got to be ready. We've got to
want it, desire it. Are you ready for revival? Let's pray. Father, we love you tonight. We
thank you for loving us. God, I pray that you'd move and work in and through the invitation
tonight. God, that we take some time to truly examine ourselves.
Lord, we pray that You do a work in each of our lives, and we'll give You the glory, which
in Christ's name we pray.
